title: filago pygmaea subsp. gaditana
intro: Filago pygmaea subsp. gaditana, also known as Dwarf Cudweed, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Dwarf Cudweed include its ability to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and reduce stress. Dwarf Cudweed can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, allowing its medicinal properties to be used in a variety of applications.
